info.title: 0xinsider API
info.description: Find your edge on Polymarket and Kalshi. Every wallet graded, every trade scored, every outlier flagged. API exposes trader grades, whale trades, smart money signals, and insider detection for AI agents, trading bots, and research tools. Normal API requests use a 30-second server timeout that returns HTTP 408 Request Timeout with an empty body when exceeded. Public REST /api/v1/* endpoints, excluding /api/v1/mcp, use Bearer-token based non-credentialed browser CORS: any Origin may call with Authorization, Content-Type, If-None-Match, Idempotency-Key, and Mcp-Session-Id request headers. Remote MCP at /api/v1/mcp is non-credentialed, but still validates Origin against the 0xinsider/localhost allowlist per MCP Streamable HTTP DNS-rebinding guidance. Successful browser CORS preflight responses advertise Access-Control-Max-Age: 86400. Browser JavaScript may read X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, Retry-After, ETag, X-Request-Id, X-Request-Cost, X-Batch-RateLimit-Limit, X-Batch-RateLimit-Remaining, X-Batch-RateLimit-Reset, Mcp-Session-Id, and X-Mcp-Error-Code response headers. Credentialed first-party routes such as /api/keys, /api/billing, and auth endpoints remain restricted to configured 0xinsider origins.

servers.description: Production (live data). Authenticate with a live key (oxi_sk_live_...); requires an active Insider subscription.

get.description: Unauthenticated API-origin discovery document pointing agents to the canonical API base URL, full docs, web-origin OpenAPI spec, health check, and representative authenticated data routes.

data.authentication: Bearer API key required for data endpoints; discovery (/api/v1), health, and /api/v1/platforms are public.

get.summary: Redirect to the canonical OpenAPI spec
get.description: Unauthenticated API-origin compatibility redirect to the canonical web-origin OpenAPI JSON document at https://0xinsider.com/api/v1/openapi.json.

307.description: Temporary redirect to the canonical web-origin OpenAPI spec.

get.description: Returns a trader's cohort-relative skill grade (S through F; calibration, risk-adjusted returns, and consistency, not profit size), P&L, win rate, and optional strategy/category data. The path accepts either an Ethereum wallet address, a known trader username, or a trd_-prefixed trader ID emitted by this API. Unknown lookups return sync_status "unknown" instead of 404.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Ethereum wallet address (0x...), known trader username, or trd_-prefixed trader ID emitted by this API.

parameters.name: expand[]
parameters.description: Backward-compatible alias for expand. Repeatable: strategy, categories, quant_metrics, trust.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Include heavy fields and trust metadata. Repeatable: strategy, categories, quant_metrics, trust.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

post.description: Returns trader intelligence for 1-25 wallet addresses or known usernames. Results preserve request order, duplicate inputs return duplicate rows, and each item is charged one batch item unit before execution. Unknown trader lookups return data with sync_status "unknown" matching the single trader endpoint.

expand.description: Shared expand flags applied to every trader item.

X-Request-Cost.description: Number of batch item units charged for this request.

X-Batch-RateLimit-Limit.description: Batch item units allowed per minute.

X-Batch-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Batch item units remaining in the current sliding window.

X-Batch-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the batch item window resets.

get.summary: Get a trader's position timeline for one market
get.description: Returns every Polymarket fill for one trader in one market, newest first, with server-computed running_amount and running_avg_price. Only HOT and WARM tier traders are tracked; other traders return 404. running_avg_price is a buy-weighted entry basis (sells do not change the running average) matching Polymarket /positions avgPrice semantics. Cursor-paginated. An id-keyed alias exists at GET /api/v1/traders/{id}/position-timeline.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Trader wallet address (0x...) or trd_-prefixed trader ID emitted by this API. Case-insensitive wallet addresses are lowercased server-side.

parameters.name: condition_id
parameters.description: Market condition_id. One timeline per (trader, market).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's next_cursor.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

get.description: Returns a trader's daily P&L time series and pre-derived stats from the precomputed daily_pnl read model: entries (daily cumulative P&L), period stats (all/90d/30d/7d), monthly aggregation, per-year totals, and the drawdown series. Reads the refreshed read model, not a per-request equity replay. A resolved trader with no daily P&L returns an empty structured object (HTTP 200); an unknown address returns 404. Spans both providers wherever the read model has the trader's series.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Wallet address (0x...), username, or trd_-prefixed trader id.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

get.summary: Get a trader's position timeline (unified identity resolver)
get.description: Unified trader-timeline route (#4975). {trader} accepts all four identity shapes - 0x... wallet, username, trd_-prefixed trader id, and bare integer id - resolved by the single shared trader-identity resolver. Returns every Polymarket fill for one trader in one market, newest first, with server-computed running_amount and running_avg_price. Only HOT and WARM tier traders are tracked; other traders return 404. Response body is byte-identical to the singular GET /api/v1/trader/{address}/position-timeline route for the same (trader, market). The prior integer-id-only variant is preserved: the same path now accepts every identity shape.

parameters.name: trader
parameters.description: Trader identity: 0x... wallet address, username, trd_-prefixed trader id, or bare integer traders.id. Resolved with precedence wallet -> trd_ -> integer -> username. Unknown identities return 404.

parameters.name: condition_id
parameters.description: Market condition_id. One timeline per (trader, market).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's next_cursor.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

get.description: Returns the current positions-board feed backed by the wallet_positions mirror. Ordered by current_value_usd DESC with deterministic (wallet, condition_id, outcome_index) tiebreakers. Pre-reconcile rows (current_value_usd IS NULL) are excluded. Cursor-paginated. Every filter pushes into SQL.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's next_cursor.

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum current position value in USD.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Exact match against provider-backed market_canonical.category.

parameters.name: min_grade
parameters.description: Minimum trader grade allowlist. `A` matches S and A; `B` matches S, A, B; etc.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Filter by the binary outcome side. `yes` maps to outcome_index=0, `no` to outcome_index=1.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

get.description: Returns the largest current open positions from graded traders, value-descending, with opaque cursor pagination. Polymarket-only by design: the large-positions scanner filters platform = 'polymarket' (backend/src/large_positions/scanner.rs:238,245), so no Kalshi rows are ever scanned and a Kalshi or unknown condition_id filter returns an empty list (never fabricated rows). The teaser cap that the internal product UI applies to anonymous viewers does not apply here: the API key already proves an active insider/annual subscription, so authed API callers get full access.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum position value in USD.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by provider-backed market category.

parameters.name: min_grade
parameters.description: Minimum trader grade.

parameters.name: condition_id
parameters.description: Scope to one market. Accepts the raw provider condition_id or the mkt_-prefixed market id (round-trips a value from a list response). Polymarket-only; a Kalshi or unknown id returns [].

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; absent on the last page.

total.description: Total ranked rows when the read model exposes a count; may be absent.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

get.description: Returns recent large trades with signal scoring. Filter by size, category, or trader grade. Filters are applied before pagination, and every request uses SQL-backed limit + 1 pagination so has_more and next_cursor reflect the filtered result set. Cursor-paginated, newest first. Market categories come from provider-backed market_canonical identity.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's next_cursor.

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum trade size in USD.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by market category (case-insensitive). A canonical bucket name (e.g. Basketball) matches every provider member that folds into it (NBA, WNBA, NCAAB); a raw provider value also resolves to its bucket.

parameters.name: min_grade
parameters.description: Minimum trader grade.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

get.description: Returns historical whale trades from local whale_alerts rows, not request-time provider fetches. Filter by condition_id, trader, category, minimum grade, platform, and RFC3339 from/to windows. All filters are pushed into SQL before LIMIT, every request uses SQL-backed limit + 1 pagination, and results are ordered newest first by traded_at desc, id desc. Metadata exposes local_replay source and best_effort completeness.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's next_cursor. Prefix: wth_. URL-encode when replaying as a query parameter.

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum trade size in USD.

parameters.name: condition_id
parameters.description: Exact raw provider condition_id. Unknown markets return an empty list.

parameters.name: trader
parameters.description: Trader wallet address, timestamp-suffixed wallet alias, or username resolved against the traders table. Unknown traders return an empty list.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by market category (case-insensitive). A canonical bucket name (e.g. Basketball) matches every provider member that folds into it (NBA, WNBA, NCAAB); a raw provider value also resolves to its bucket.

parameters.name: min_grade
parameters.description: Minimum trader grade.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter by whale_alerts.platform. all is equivalent to omitted.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive RFC3339 lower bound on whale_alerts.traded_at.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Exclusive RFC3339 upper bound on whale_alerts.traded_at. Must be after from when both are present.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

get.description: Returns ranked traders (grades S/A/B) sorted by score descending. Supports cursor pagination and optional category/strategy filters.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor (score_address format).

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by category. Values are matched to canonical category buckets: political variants (Elections, Global Politics, U.S. Politics, ...) fold into Politics, Geopolitics stays distinct, Culture/Entertainment map to Pop Culture, Science maps to Science & Tech, and Finance/Business map to Stocks. Mapped buckets are case-insensitive; passthrough categories (Crypto, NBA, and the sports leagues) match case-sensitively against the provider-native bucket key, so use exact casing (e.g. Crypto, NBA).

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: Filter by ML-detected strategy type. Values come from backend/src/trader_analysis/classification/decision_tree.rs and are matched exactly against trader_classifications.primary_type. Unknown values currently match zero rows; the handler does not return HTTP 400.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

ETag.description: Stable validator for the current leaderboard payload. Re-send it via If-None-Match for conditional GETs.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current leaderboard payload.

ETag.description: Validator for the unchanged leaderboard payload.

get.description: Returns wallets ranked by trailing-window realized P&L (Polymarket-only discovery), with opaque page-cursor pagination. Each row carries window P&L, volume, distinct markets, grade, hot-streak tier, and a zero-filled daily P&L series. The underlying read model is warmed into Redis; a cold read returns 503 (warming, retry-after), never a 500 or a fabricated ranking. Polymarket-only: the read model filters platform = 'polymarket'.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Trailing window.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; absent on the last page.

total.description: Total ranked rows when the read model exposes a count; may be absent.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

parameters.name: q

parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Browse whale-active titled markets with category, platform, status, and keyword filters. Paginates visible discovery entries rather than raw market rows, returns live category/platform facets alongside grouped event clusters or standalone markets, and includes total on the first page only. Categories come straight from provider metadata (Polymarket Gamma, Kalshi) and facets are flat value/label/count rows.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by market category (case-insensitive). A canonical bucket name (e.g. Basketball) matches every provider member that folds into it (NBA, WNBA, NCAAB); a raw provider value also resolves to its bucket. Facet values are returned as the canonical bucket.

get.description: Ranks markets by absolute net S/A/B-grade whale flow over a requested timeframe. Use this discovery endpoint to answer where smart money is flowing before drilling into a specific market with /api/v1/market/{condition_id}/intel. Pagination is anchored by an opaque cursor carrying the first page as_of timestamp so new whale trades do not reorder page 2.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Lookback window for grade-filtered whale flow aggregation.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from previous response's next_cursor. Encodes the first-page as_of timestamp plus the last row's absolute net flow and condition_id.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by market category (case-insensitive). A canonical bucket name (e.g. Basketball) matches every provider member that folds into it (NBA, WNBA, NCAAB); a raw provider value also resolves to its bucket.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter by source platform. all is a request-side no-op.

parameters.name: min_grade
parameters.description: Minimum latest trader grade included in the flow. Default B means S/A/B only; unranked traders are excluded.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Optional post-aggregate flow direction filter.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

market.title: Will BTC close above $100k this week?

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current payload.

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of the live feed envelopes the platform already broadcasts (whale-trade pulses and other public/insider feed events). Forwards the same backend-owned envelope shape as the internal feed; no provider data is recomputed. Authenticated via the oxi_sk Bearer key like every other /api/v1 endpoint, and limited to a small number of concurrent connections per API key and a cluster-wide ceiling across all keys (HTTP 429 with Retry-After when either cap is exceeded; HTTP 503 with Retry-After if the admission backend is briefly unavailable). Each delivered frame carries an SSE id equal to the envelope sequence; reconnect with the Last-Event-ID header (or the last_event_id / seq query fallback) to replay the missed window before resuming live. When the requested resume point predates the retained window the stream emits one resync marker event (event: resync) instead of silently skipping frames. Idle connections receive periodic ': keep-alive' comment lines. This is a long-lived response: keep the connection open and read frames as they arrive.

parameters.name: Last-Event-ID
parameters.description: Sequence id of the last frame the client successfully processed. The stream replays the strictly-newer window before resuming live. Browser EventSource clients send this automatically on reconnect. Omit to attach live from now.

parameters.name: last_event_id
parameters.description: Query-string fallback for the Last-Event-ID resume cursor when the client cannot set the request header. Numeric sequence id; non-numeric or absent attaches live with no replay.

parameters.name: seq
parameters.description: Alias for last_event_id. Numeric sequence id to resume after; non-numeric or absent attaches live with no replay.

200.description: Open Server-Sent Events stream. The body is an unbounded text/event-stream of frames; each data frame is a JSON feed envelope (with seq, published_at, type, and event-specific fields) and carries an SSE id equal to seq. resync marker frames use event: resync.

schema.description:
Newline-delimited SSE frames: 'id: <seq>
data: <json-envelope>

' for events, 'event: resync
id: <seq>
data: <json>

' for resync markers, and ': keep-alive' comment lines while idle.

resyncMarker.summary: Resync marker after a too-old resume point

get.description: Returns durable public whale-trade intelligence events strictly after an opaque cursor backed by whale_alerts.id. This is a separate API-key contract from the browser/session /api/events/feed stream: browser-only and private alert, following, radar, and position patch events are excluded until they have a durable public outbox.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque event replay cursor returned as next_cursor by a prior response. The cursor maps to whale_alerts.id and is valid across backend replicas. Omit to fetch the latest durable public suffix.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum durable public whale-trade events to return.

get.description: Returns webhook destinations owned by the authenticated API key user. Disabled endpoints are omitted.

post.description: Creates a pending HTTPS webhook destination. The response includes one-time signing_secret and verification.token values. Deliveries are not sent until the endpoint is verified.

get.description: Returns one webhook destination owned by the authenticated API key user.

patch.description: Updates name, HTTPS URL, event types, or enabled state. URL changes force pending_verification and return a new verification token.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a webhook destination owned by the authenticated API key user. Existing delivery audit rows remain retained.

post.description: Activates a pending webhook destination when the one-time verification token matches and has not expired.

post.summary: Rotate a builder webhook signing secret
post.description: Rotates the endpoint signing secret and returns the new signing_secret once in the response.

get.description: Returns API health status. No authentication required. Limited to 60 requests per minute per IP.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

ETag.description: Stable validator for the current health payload. Re-send it via If-None-Match for conditional GETs.

background_jobs.description: Public-safe aggregate status for expected background job successful-completion heartbeats. Does not expose Redis keys, job names, raw errors, provider payloads, wallet addresses, or condition IDs.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current health payload.

ETag.description: Validator for the unchanged health payload.

post.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Streamable HTTP transport. Accepts a JSON-RPC 2.0 request and returns a JSON-RPC response. Supported methods: initialize, notifications/initialized, ping, tools/list, tools/call. Remote MCP exposes 29 read-only tools for public V1 read operations: get_leaderboard, get_trader, batch_get_traders, get_whale_trades, get_whale_trade, get_whale_trades_history, get_market_intel, batch_get_market_intel, get_smart_money_flows, get_market_snapshot, get_insider_radar, get_insider_radar_flag, get_positions, get_position_timeline, get_position_timeline_by_id, search_markets, explore_markets, get_event_replay_since, list_webhooks, get_webhook, get_daily_report_snapshot, get_weekly_report_snapshot, get_monthly_report_snapshot, get_report, get_trader_export_snapshot, get_platforms, get_large_positions, get_trending_wallets, get_trader_pnl. Webhook create/update/delete/verify/rotate operations are intentionally not exposed as remote MCP tools. The remote endpoint advertises tools only; it does not implement resources/list, resources/read, prompts/list, or prompts/get. Each tool dispatches to the matching /api/v1/* handler in-process so auth, rate limits, and payload shape match. Auth should use Authorization: Bearer <token>. ?token=<token> remains a legacy compatibility path for URL-only MCP clients, but URL secrets can land in shell history, browser history, and logs, so prefer headers or the stdio package. Mcp-Session-Id is minted on initialize and echoed on every response. Origin header, when present, is validated against the 0xinsider + localhost allowlist.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Deprecated compatibility API-key query parameter for remote MCP clients that cannot send Authorization headers. Prefer Authorization: Bearer <token> or the stdio package because URL secrets are easier to leak through logs and history.

parameters.name: Mcp-Session-Id
parameters.description: Session ID minted by the server on initialize; echoed on every subsequent request.

get.description: Opens a text/event-stream connection for server-initiated notifications as described in the MCP Streamable HTTP transport. The endpoint currently sends no notifications — clients that only care about request/response use POST.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: API key as query parameter (same contract as POST).

parameters.name: Mcp-Session-Id
parameters.description: Session ID minted by the server on initialize.

get.description: Unified convenience route (#4975) that consolidates the three singular report routes. Dispatches to the exact per-granularity cap (daily 50, weekly 100, monthly 200) and date window the legacy /api/v1/reports/{daily,weekly,monthly} routes use, so the response body is byte-identical to the matching legacy route for the same period. The three legacy routes stay live. Also reachable via the MCP get_report selector.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: Report granularity selector.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Period token for the granularity. daily: UTC date YYYY-MM-DD. weekly: ISO week YYYY-WW, or a from,to YYYY-MM-DD pair. monthly: UTC month YYYY-MM.

get.description: Returns a dated daily whale-activity report snapshot with source_range, snapshot.status, completeness, and reconciliation metadata. Report whale volume is local whale-alert activity volume, not provider lifetime trader volume.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: UTC report date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

get.description: Returns a weekly whale-activity report snapshot. Pass either from/to UTC dates or an ISO YYYY-WW week token. The response identifies closed ranges as final and current ranges as rolling.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: UTC source-range start in YYYY-MM-DD format; required with to.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: UTC source-range end in YYYY-MM-DD format; required with from.

parameters.name: week
parameters.description: ISO week selector in YYYY-WW format; alternative to from/to.

get.description: Returns a UTC monthly whale-activity report snapshot with source_range, completeness, and reconciliation metadata.

parameters.name: month
parameters.description: UTC report month in YYYY-MM format.

get.description: Returns export source-range, completeness, volume reconciliation, row-count estimate, and large-export policy for one trader. V1 is metadata-only; full raw dataset export remains on the internal streaming or async export route.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Trader wallet address (0x...), known trader username-style lookup, or trd_-prefixed trader ID emitted by this API.

get.summary: Inspect current API usage without spending primary request quota
get.description: Returns the authenticated caller sliding-window request budget and UTC-day usage. This endpoint is authenticated and does not increment the primary Redis rate-limit counter or log itself into the API usage table; it is separately throttled at 100 reads/minute per user to protect the usage-count query.

200.description: Usage budget and counters for the authenticated API key owner.

get.summary: Get today's Pick of the Day
get.description: One sourced sharp-money call a day (Insider-tier). Returns the single latest published pick: the backed side, the pre-game odds and $100 return, the proven smart-money holders on that side, the grade and category edge, and the thesis. The price is snapshotted before kickoff so it does not drift. Returns 404 when no pick is published.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

ETag.description: Stable validator for the current Pick of the Day payload. Re-send it via If-None-Match for conditional GETs.

data.thesis: Graded soccer specialists are stacked on Portugal at a price the market has not fully repriced.
data.disclaimer: Not financial advice. Prediction markets carry risk; do your own research.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current Pick of the Day payload.

ETag.description: Validator for the unchanged Pick of the Day payload.

data.thesis: Graded soccer specialists are stacked on Portugal at a price the market has not fully repriced.
data.disclaimer: Not financial advice. Prediction markets carry risk; do your own research.

get.summary: Get the Pick of the Day track record
get.description: Every published Pick of the Day with its real outcome, plus the rolling hit rate (wins / decided; void and pending excluded). Resolved picks are public; a still-pending pick's backed side is included for the authenticated Insider key.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Conditional GET validator from a previous ETag. Matching values return 304 Not Modified with an empty body.

200.description: Pick of the Day track record

ETag.description: Stable validator for the current Pick of the Day archive payload. Re-send it via If-None-Match for conditional GETs.

304.description: Not Modified. Returned when If-None-Match matches the current Pick of the Day archive payload.

ETag.description: Validator for the unchanged Pick of the Day archive payload.

bearerAuth.description: API key authentication. Send your key in the Authorization header as `Bearer oxi_sk_live_...`. Live keys require an active Insider subscription and return live data.

WebhookId.name: id
WebhookId.description: Webhook endpoint id owned by the authenticated API key user.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Optional safe-retry key. Reuse the same value only when retrying the exact same mutation request body; a different body returns 422 and an in-flight matching request returns 409.

grade.description: All-time trader grade (S, A, B, C, D, F).

state.description: Always 'full' for an authenticated Insider key.

release_at.description: The pick's nominal daily release slot (fixed 19:00 Europe/Berlin -- CEST/CET). The actual publish instant can trail it by minutes to hours when no candidate qualified at the slot and the selector kept retrying (retries stop when the day's kickoff-eligibility window closes at 21:00 US/Eastern).

is_locked.description: Whether the market has locked (kicked off) so the snapshotted price no longer trades.

outcome.description: Settlement outcome of the backed side; 'pending' until the market resolves.

token_id.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for the backed outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

position.description: The backed side phrased as a bet (e.g. "Portugal to win").

side_summary.description: One-line summary of which side smart money is backing.

smart_wallet_count.description: Number of proven smart-money wallets on the backed side.

top_grade.description: Highest trader grade among the backed-side holders (S, A, B, C, D, F).

category_edge_pct.description: Category win-rate edge as a fraction: the backed-side cohort's win rate in this category minus the non-market-maker category baseline (e.g. 0.09 = +9 points). Present only when statistically credible; null/absent otherwise (and for legacy rows). Paired with category_edge_sample.

category_edge_sample.description: Pooled count of resolved markets behind category_edge_pct (the headline's n). Present if and only if category_edge_pct is present.

smart_usd.description: Recency-weighted graded-flow magnitude in USD; omitted when <= 0.

backed_price.description: Pre-game snapshot probability (0..1) for the backed side.

return_per_100.description: Gross return on a $100 stake at the snapshotted price (100 / backed_price).

sharp_pct.description: Backed-side smart-money dollar consensus as a fraction 0..1: the share of the sharp dollars on the backed side. A conviction signal, NOT a probability or expected-value claim. Frozen at generation.

market_pct.description: Market-implied probability of the backed side as a fraction 0..1 (equals backed_price), re-exposed alongside sharp_pct for the WHY breakdown.

consensus_edge_pct.description: Consensus edge = sharp_pct - market_pct, the conviction-vs-price gap (how much more of the smart money sits on this side than the price implies). This is NOT an expected-value or guaranteed edge. Null when either input is null.

traders.description: Count of proven smart-money wallets on the backed side (equals smart_wallet_count).

backed_sharp_usd.description: Raw backed-side smart-money USD frozen at generation (the 'Sharp $'), NOT the recency-weighted smart_usd which decays.

holders.description: Proven smart-money holders on the backed side.

holder_count.description: True total of proven holders on the backed side (may exceed the holders array length).

editorial_note.description: Optional editorial note attached to the pick.

event_slug.description: The canonical /event game-page slug (one neutral page per game), null when the game has no neutral event page.

sports_context.description: Provider-first sports context for the pick's market (team logos, league branding, live score). Full-state only; omitted when the pick is not a team-sports market.

disclaimer.description: Risk disclaimer shown with every pick.

PickSportsContext.description: Provider-first sports context for a Pick of the Day market: team crests, league branding, and live score. Team logos and league logo are provider-owned (Polymarket /teams crests for clubs, country flags for national teams and tennis players); no local derivation.

league_name.description: League or competition display name (e.g. "Premier League").

yes_team.description: The team mapped to the market's YES outcome, or the parent-event home/first team when event_matchup is true.

no_team.description: The team mapped to the market's NO outcome, or the parent-event away/second team when event_matchup is true.

game_id.description: Provider game identifier (Polymarket Gamma gameId); omitted when null.

event_matchup.description: Always present. True when the two teams are the parent-event match identity for a teamless binary leg (e.g. a draw, totals, or prop market), not the market's own outcomes.

event_subject_team.description: Present only alongside event_matchup: the event team the binary leg is about (provider group_item_title matched to a matchup team, e.g. Belgium for "Will Belgium win?"), i.e. the winner on a Yes resolution. Omitted for teamless legs (draw, totals, prop).

PickSportsTeam.description: A single sports team or competitor in a Pick of the Day market's sports context. Every field is provider-owned and nullable.

label.description: Team display label as it appears on the market outcome (e.g. "Portugal").

full_name.description: Full team or competitor name (e.g. "Portugal national football team").

logo.description: Team crest or flag URL (provider-owned: Polymarket /teams crest for clubs, country flag for national teams and tennis players).

color.description: Team brand color as a hex string (provider-owned).

record.description: Win-loss record as a display string (e.g. "12-4").

score.description: Live or final score as a display string when the game is in play or settled.

picks.description: Every published Pick of the Day, most recent last.

pick_outcome_label.description: The backed side's outcome label. Omitted for a still-pending pick when the request is not from an authenticated Insider key.

top_grade.description: Best grade among the proven smart-money holders on the backed side (S, A, B, C, D, F).

outcome.description: Settlement outcome of the backed side; 'pending' until the market resolves.

return_per_100.description: Gross return on a $100 stake on this resolved pick: a win returns 100 / backed_price, a loss returns 0, a void refunds 100. Omitted (not null) for a still-pending pick or a resolved pick with no frozen price; mirrors the backend skip-when-absent behavior and the route-client optional (non-nullable) schema.

wins.description: Number of decided picks that won.

losses.description: Number of decided picks that lost.

decided.description: Number of decided picks (wins + losses); excludes void and pending.

pct.description: Rolling hit rate as a percentage (wins / decided * 100, to 1 decimal); 0 when none are decided.

void.description: Number of picks that resolved void (excluded from the hit rate).

pending.description: Number of picks still pending resolution (excluded from the hit rate).

net_profit_usd.description: Cumulative profit (USD) of a $100/pick strategy over resolved, priced picks: a win pays 100/backed_price - 100, a loss pays -100, a void pays 0. A resolved pick with no frozen price is excluded.

staked_usd.description: Total staked (USD) = 100 * count of win/loss picks with a frozen price (void excluded -- a void refunds the stake).

roi_pct.description: Return on the staked amount as a percentage (net_profit_usd / staked_usd * 100, to 1 decimal); 0 when nothing is staked.

series.description: Cumulative track-record series, one point per decided (win/loss) pick in ascending pick_date order (void and pending add no point). The last point's net_profit_usd and hit_rate_pct equal the headline net_profit_usd and pct by construction. Empty when nothing is decided.

net_profit_usd.description: Running cumulative $100/pick profit (USD) through this pick (priced win/loss only; an unpriced win/loss carries it forward).

hit_rate_pct.description: Running rolling hit rate (wins / decided * 100, to 1 decimal) through this pick.

token.description: One-time verification token returned only on create or URL change.

signing_secret.description: Returned only on create or rotate-secret.

url.description: Public HTTPS callback URL. Local/private/internal targets are rejected.

cache_age_s.description: Cache age in seconds, null if not cached.

api_base_url.description: Canonical API origin for public V1 requests.

authentication.const: Bearer API key required for data endpoints; discovery (/api/v1), health, and /api/v1/platforms are public.

authenticated_routes.description: Representative authenticated data routes. Full route coverage lives in OpenAPI and llms-full.txt.

cache_age_s.description: Cache age in seconds, null if not cached.

reason.description: Explains that local replay completeness can vary by market and time window.

id.description: Opaque event ID; currently identical to cursor for the whale_alerts.id sequence.

TrustSource.description: Source metadata for a trust-critical value. Providers and DB/read models own business truth; clients should not infer missing provider facts from titles, slugs, zeros, or empty arrays.

owner.description: Provider, table/read-model, cache, or service that owns the value.

field.description: Provider field, DB column, or computed field name when applicable.

TrustFreshness.description: Freshness metadata for a trust-critical value. This is separate from transport cache fields in ResponseMeta.

TrustReconciliation.description: How provider-owned facts were reconciled with stored/read-model values.

TrustCompleteness.description: Whether the described value or result set is complete for its stated contract.

TrustMetadata.description: Shared source/freshness/reconciliation/completeness metadata for public API values that may be cached, stale, partial, computed, or provider-unavailable. Unavailable provider values must be represented with explicit metadata instead of fabricated zeros or empty arrays.

TraderTrust.description: Field-level trust metadata returned only when GET /api/v1/trader/{address} includes expand=trust.

event_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the on-chain fill.

token_id.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for this outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

amount_delta.description: Signed share delta (+ on buy, − on sell).

price.description: Fill price in USDC per share, in [0,1].

usdc_notional.description: Positive USDC notional of the fill.

tx_hash.description: Polygon transaction hash of the fill.

running_amount.description: Cumulative signed share balance after this fill.

running_avg_price.description: Buy-weighted entry basis (matches Polymarket /positions avgPrice semantics). Sells do not change this value. 0 when no buys have occurred yet.

streak_tier.description: Hot-streak tier (trailing-7d cross-sectional percentile); a separate axis from the all-time grade. Null when no recent activity.

category_strengths.description: Per-category performance breakdown (expand=categories or expand[]=categories). Null unless expanded. Object keyed by category name; each value is the precomputed trader_rankings.category_ranks payload (rank, total_in_category, total_pnl, scaled_total_pnl, n_markets, wins, losses, win_rate; scaled_total_pnl is a legacy alias that currently equals total_pnl). Pass-through DB JSON: keys and value shape are DB-owned, so the inner shape is intentionally unconstrained and may carry additional compatibility fields.

quant_metrics.description: Curated advanced risk/performance metrics (expand=quant_metrics or expand[]=quant_metrics). Omitted unless expanded and the trader has computed metrics; when present, all listed fields are present (each is a number or null). null means insufficient trade history and must not be treated as 0. This is a fixed, documented field set, refreshed periodically by the cross-sectional ranking job.

smart_score.description: Composite skill score, 0-100. smart_score = clamp(0, 100, 30*sharpe_percentile_fraction + 20*profit_factor_percentile_fraction + 20*edge_consistency_percentile_fraction + 10*min(1, return_on_capital/2) + 10*equity_smoothness + 10*(1 - min(1, asset_concentration))). Higher is better. null when insufficient history.

copy_score.description: Copyability score, 0-100. Same base as smart_score minus penalties for traits that make a strategy hard to replicate: -20 if fewer than 50 markets traded, -15 if positions are highly concentrated, -15 if position sizing exceeds about 2x Kelly, -10 if the worst single-trade loss exceeds 30%, -10 if edge is inconsistent; result clamped to 0-100. Higher means easier to follow. null when insufficient history.

sharpe_30d.description: Sharpe ratio over the trailing 30 days (risk-adjusted return; higher is better). Magnitude can be large for small samples. null when insufficient history.

sharpe_7d.description: Sharpe ratio over the trailing 7 days (risk-adjusted return; higher is better). null when insufficient history.

profit_factor.description: Gross profit divided by gross loss; greater than 1 is profitable. Capped at 1000 when there are effectively no losses. null when insufficient history.

edge_consistency.description: Stability of the trader's edge over time, 0-1 (higher is more consistent). null when insufficient history.

sharpe_percentile.description: Cross-sectional percentile rank of the trader's Sharpe ratio versus all traders, 0-100. null when insufficient history.

pf_percentile.description: Cross-sectional percentile rank of profit factor versus all traders, 0-100. null when insufficient history.

consistency_percentile.description: Cross-sectional percentile rank of edge consistency versus all traders, 0-100. null when insufficient history.

trust.description: Field-level trust metadata. Present only when expand=trust or expand[]=trust is requested.

platform.description: Provider discriminator. Slice 5 ships Polymarket only; the field stays in the response shape so Kalshi positions can land without a breaking change.

side.description: Binary outcome side. Non-binary positions are not surfaced on V1.

token_id.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for this outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

shares.description: Live share count from the wallet_positions mirror.

avg_price.description: Volume-weighted entry price for this leg.

current_value_usd.description: Current mark-to-market value in USD (always non-null on V1 — pre-reconcile rows are excluded).

cash_pnl.description: Unrealized P&L for the open position (Polymarket `cashPnl`).

last_reconciled_at.description: Max updated_at across mirror legs for this pair.

freshness.description: Backend-computed staleness bucket derived from last_reconciled_at.

is_new_wallet.description: Wallet is younger than the new-wallet threshold (30 days).

outcome_label.description: Provider-reported outcome label (e.g. team name for sports). Separate from `side` because provider labels can diverge from the binary Yes/No axis.

outcome.description: Traded outcome label (e.g. "Yes"/"No"/team name), resolved provider-first from the trade's outcome_index against market_canonical (index 0 -> yes, 1 -> no). Distinct axis from side (BUY/SELL): side is the trade direction, outcome is which leg was traded. null for multi-outcome (outcome_index >= 2) or unsynced markets; a Kalshi row carries its provider label here (only token_id is null for Kalshi, since there is no CLOB token).

token_id.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for the traded outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

streak_tier.description: Hot-streak tier (trailing-7d cross-sectional percentile); a separate axis from the all-time grade. Null when no recent activity.

url_slug.description: First-party market page slug used for internal links.

token_id_yes.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for the YES outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

token_id_no.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for the NO outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

outcome_yes_label.description: Display label for the YES/outcome_index=0 side, enriched from provider outcome metadata when available.

outcome_no_label.description: Display label for the NO/outcome_index=1 side, enriched from provider outcome metadata when available.

outcome_yes_provider_id.description: Provider-owned YES/outcome_index=0 identifier when available for trade-ticket wiring.

outcome_no_provider_id.description: Provider-owned NO/outcome_index=1 identifier when available for trade-ticket wiring.

discover_score.description: Backend-owned deterministic market discovery score used by the hot sort.

token_id.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for the net-flow direction outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).
The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for this outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).
The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for the net-flow direction outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

MarketSnapshotTrust.description: Price and spread trust metadata returned only when GET /api/v1/market/{condition_id}/snapshot includes expand=trust.

trust.description: Price and spread trust metadata. Present only when expand=trust or expand[]=trust is requested.

reset.description: Unix timestamp when the batch item window resets.

request_cost.description: Number of batch item units reserved before execution.

index.description: Zero-based request index. Duplicate inputs keep separate result rows.
Zero-based request index. Duplicate inputs keep separate result rows.

outcome.description: Provider outcome label for the traded side.

side.description: Trade direction (BUY or SELL), not the outcome side.

token_id.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for the traded outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

pseudonym.description: Trader pseudonym, when no display name is known.

daily_usage.description: UTC-day usage count. Current V1 has no daily hard cap, so limit and remaining are null rather than synthesized.

platform.description: Provider discriminator. Always polymarket today (scanner is Polymarket-only); kept in the shape so Kalshi can land without a breaking change.

total_size_usd.description: Aggregate provider currentValue for this position leg in USD.

position_unrealized_pnl.description: Provider per-position unrealized P&L (trader_markets.unrealized_pnl_num, from Polymarket cashPnl).

share_count.description: Live share count for this position.

current_price.description: Latest provider mark price for the position's token.

outcome_label.description: Backend-resolved outcome label (provider outcome, else Yes/No from the binary index).

token_id.description: The Polymarket CLOB token id (ERC1155 asset id, decimal string) for this outcome; null when unavailable (e.g. Kalshi markets, unsynced markets).

event_leg_count.description: Legs collapsed into this representative row for one (wallet, event, outcome side) group. 1 means standalone.

event_total_value_usd.description: Aggregate provider currentValue across collapsed sibling legs; equals total_size_usd when event_leg_count = 1.

pnl.description: Trader lifetime realized P&L across all markets.

rank.description: 1-based rank within the full ranked set (stable across pages).

platform.description: Real provider platform; surfaced, never coerced. Polymarket-only today.

trending_pnl_usd.description: Realized P&L over the trailing window in USD (ranking axis).

grade.description: All-time cohort-relative skill grade (forecasting calibration, risk-adjusted returns, consistency); a separate axis from streak_tier. Not a profit ranking, and relative, so it drifts as the cohort moves. Omitted when the trader is Unranked (fewer than 5 markets, insufficient track record to cohort-rank).

streak_tier.description: Hot-streak tier (trailing-7d cross-sectional percentile). Null when no recent activity.

daily_pnl_series.description: Zero-filled daily P&L series across the window.

RequestTimeout.description: Request exceeded the server's 30-second transport timeout. The timeout response has an empty body because it is generated before handler-level JSON error shaping.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded (100 req/min; batch endpoints also reserve 100 batch item units/min before execution)

RateLimitUnavailable.description: Redis-backed authenticated rate limiter unavailable; retry after the per-process outage cooldown

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the middleware will probe the Redis-backed rate limiter again.

IdempotencyInProgress.description: An Idempotency-Key request with the same body is still in progress. Retry shortly with the same key and body.

error.message: Idempotency-Key request is still in progress; retry shortly

IdempotencyConflict.description: The Idempotency-Key was already used with a different request body.

different_body.summary: Idempotency key reused with a different body

error.message: Idempotency-Key already used with a different request body

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Authenticated V1 per-user request limit for the current sliding window.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Authenticated V1 requests remaining in the current sliding window after this response.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the authenticated V1 request window resets.

X-Request-Id.description: Server-generated request identifier for support and tracing.

ETag.description: Strong validator for conditional GET. Send as If-None-Match to receive 304 when unchanged.

tags.name: Traders

tags.name: Positions

tags.name: Large Positions

tags.name: Whale Trades
tags.description: Recent and historical large trade intelligence.

tags.name: Leaderboard

tags.description: Signed builder webhook destinations and delivery controls.

tags.name: Usage
tags.description: Developer API budget and usage introspection.
